---
title: "Blue Butterwort (Pinguicula caerulea)"
slug: "pinguicula-caerulea"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/621795611/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Blue Butterwort"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Lamiales"
tax_family: "Lentibulariaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Lamiales", "Lentibulariaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/621795611/medium.jpg" alt="Blue Butterwort" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/damien6868">
damien6868</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/341548515">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Lamiales
-   **Family:** Lentibulariaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/341548515)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Uncommon/Rare species in Florida found in moist, nutrient-poor habitats.

## Natural history notes

A small carnivorous plant characterized by its basal rosette of flat, sticky leaves and delicate blue to violet flowers held on slender stalks. In Florida, it is found in moist, nutrient-poor habitats such as bogs, wet flatwoods, and seepage areas. It typically blooms during the cooler to warmer months and captures small insects on its sticky leaves to supplement nutrients.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/166884-Pinguicula-caerulea)
-   [Local profile](https://www.flawildflowers.org/flower-friday-pinguicula-caerulea/)
